string.text: Designed for high-quality field repair of military equipment.
ORK-42 Army Tools
Intended for adjustment and calibration of military equipment.
RKB-86u Army Tools
Universal Pilot Key
Designed to provide emergency access to helicopters.
Professional toolset for fine repair and modification of detectors. The included items are sufficient for any work related to adjustment and upgrading of all types of detectors.
Detector Work Tools
Scientific Report No. 1264
Several A4 pages stapled together. Cover inscription:\n"Fusion of Anomalous Rocks"\nS/N 1264
SBU Data Cell No. 15764

Attached information: A mercenary group detained our agent near Jupiter. There is reason to believe the agent is still alive. Your objective is to rescue the agent and neutralize the resisting mercenaries. For safe entry into the camp, use the "Mercenary Suit".\nTo upload data into the PDA, usage is required.
Item No. 1964

Top secret information: As locations are visited, additional transit channels will be unlocked:
 %c[255,238,153,26]Ģ %c[default] Zaton - Jupiter
 %c[255,238,153,26]Ģ %c[default] Jupiter - Zaton
 %c[255,238,153,26]Ģ %c[default] Jupiter - Pripyat
 %c[255,238,153,26]Ģ %c[default] Pripyat - Jupiter
 %c[255,238,153,26]Ģ %c[default] Jupiter - Underground
 %c[255,238,153,26]Ģ %c[default] Pripyat - Underground
 %c[255,238,153,26]Ģ %c[default] Pripyat - Zaton
 %c[255,238,153,26]Ģ %c[default] Zaton - Pripyat
 %c[default]To upload data into the PDA, usage is required.
%c[207,155,80,255]This action is irreversible. Think carefully before using!
Letter from the Liaison
The letter reads: To Major Degtyaryov. You must urgently meet with the liaison at the power plant territory. Call sign - "Siren Wailing".
Messler Optic Flashlight
Halogen flashlight combined with an optical sight in a single housing.
Case with Ruger GP100
The case contains a disassembled Ruger GP100 and a repair kit for it.
CCA72 Medkit
Used by the Ministry of Emergency Situations, a universal medical kit with a defibrillator.
Suzanna Camera
Medium-format analog camera with a rigidly attached tripod.
KhemulÆs Medallion
Inscription on the medallion: Group B117.
ōBaileysö
A rare drink, at least within the Zone.
High-Frequency Radio ōGrundigö
Used for communication over vast distances. Made in Germany.
ōPathfinderö Binoculars
Specialized military binoculars. Standard issue for military snipers.
Living Water
Another miracle born within the Zone.
V98 Medkit
Large medical kit. Excellent for treating any wounds or bleeding.
Strange Note 1

The letter reads: "...youÆre the first to see me... I saved your life... in exchange for silence and... and help. Help me... I canÆt appear among people, but I know the scientists surely have what I need... NG-7. Bring me this medicine. It will help me. And I will repay you."\nTo upload data into the PDA, usage is required.
Strange Note 2
The letter reads: "Thank you. Check my locker at the end of the corridor. Your reward is there."
NG-7 Vaccine
Ozersky said nothing about this medicine, but I assume this medicine stops the mutation.
Alpha Squad CommanderÆs PDA

%c[255,238,153,26]Commander of the Auxiliary Group.%c[default] Order to all auxiliary groups! Provide support to special forces groups or, if possible, capture alive or dead Alexander Grigoryevich Degtyaryov, born 1978, native of Pripyat, USSR. Description: slender build, looks 25-30 years old, height 179 cm, wavy light brown hair. Highly dangerous, SBU Major. All items found on him must be delivered immediately to headquarters. Pay special attention to the preservation of PDAs, paper and electronic data carriers. Five times the reward for successful completion!
Alpha Squad CommanderÆs PDA

%c[255,238,153,26]Commander of the Special Purpose Group.%c[default] Order to all special purpose groups! Capture alive or dead Alexander Grigoryevich Degtyaryov, born 1978, native of Pripyat, USSR. Description: slender build, looks 25-30 years old, height 179 cm, wavy light brown hair. Highly dangerous, SBU Major. All items found on him must be delivered immediately to headquarters. Pay special attention to the preservation of PDAs, paper and electronic data carriers. Ten times the reward for successful completion!
Alpha Squad CommanderÆs PDA

%c[255,238,153,26]Commander of the Special Purpose Group.%c[default] Order to all auxiliary and special purpose groups! Capture alive or dead Alexander Grigoryevich Degtyaryov, born 1978, native of Pripyat, USSR. Description: slender build, looks 25-30 years old, height 179 cm, wavy light brown hair. Highly dangerous, SBU Major. All items found on him must be delivered immediately to headquarters. Pay special attention to the preservation of PDAs, paper and electronic data carriers. Fifteen times the reward for successful completion!
Alpha Squad CommanderÆs PDA

%c[255,238,153,26]Commander of the Special Elite Unit.%c[default] Order to all special elite unit groups! Capture alive or dead Alexander Grigoryevich Degtyaryov, born 1978, native of Pripyat, USSR. Description: slender build, looks 25-30 years old, height 179 cm, wavy light brown hair. Highly dangerous, SBU Major. All items found on him must be delivered immediately to headquarters. Pay special attention to the preservation of PDAs, paper and electronic data carriers. Twenty times the reward for successful completion!
Alpha Squad CommanderÆs PDA

%c[255,238,153,26]Commander of the Special Ultra-Elite Unit.%c[default] Order to all special ultra-elite unit groups! Capture alive or dead Alexander Grigoryevich Degtyaryov, born 1978, native of Pripyat, USSR. Description: slender build, looks 25-30 years old, height 179 cm, wavy light brown hair. Highly dangerous, SBU Major. All items found on him must be delivered immediately to headquarters. Pay special attention to the preservation of PDAs, paper and electronic data carriers. Thirty times the reward for successful completion!
Bulldog PDA
PDA containing valuable information for stalkers.
Renegade PDA
PDA containing valuable information for stalkers.
Renegade PDA
PDA containing valuable information for stalkers.
Renegade PDA
PDA containing valuable information for stalkers.
KasilovÆs PDA
PDA containing unknown information.
ōChernyö PDA
PDA of the mercenary leader. PDA info: Our password is "IÆm in distress".
ōLeonö Case
Case intercepted by a Monolith agent from mercenaries. Leon requested the case to be delivered to Pripyat unopened.
ōVoronö PDA
PDA contains an archived file protected by a password. My PDA does not support this archiver version. It must be given to Gromoboy Ś he might be able to extract the information.
Personalized ōBeretta92FSö
Personalized weapon. Custom made, with the inscription "Chapa" on the grip.
"Acid Infusorium"
Unique artifact with excellent protective properties. It must be delivered to the Transmuters... what a pity.
Parcel for the Saver
According to stalker Petka Razmandyay, inside is a subscription fee. Recipient Ś Markovich, saver on Skadovsk.
Parcel for Kanevsky
What the Avenger dismissively called "fireworks" actually looks like a neatly packed C-4 charge with a detonator. Recipient Ś "Duty" Kanevsky.
Trophy "Ingram Mac-10"
The submachine gunÆs grip is engraved. It can only be partially read: "For excellent ... service..." and someoneÆs initials "A.S. P.shk.n".
Trophy "AK-47"
Old worn-out assault rifle, with claw marks on the stock. A weapon of legend. They say Marked One himself killed the last person in the Zone of "fracture" with it.
Trophy "Mossberg 590Mk.L"

Judging by its appearance, this weapon has been pressed into the ground for a long time Ś most likely near the "Agroprom". The factory marking on the stock says it is a limited batch shotgun with superior bluing.
Trophy "Steyr AUG SS190"

A weapon for true special forces. A technician who knew his job worked on this weapon, significantly improving its combat characteristics. Rumor has it this was Shurup Ś the legendary craftsman from the "Military Warehouses".
"Seraphim" Detector

A special scientific development. Equipped with a locator for anomalous formations and an LED display showing the direction to an unknown specimen. It can detect specimens and measure their distance. The specimen search mode activates by lifting the antenna-lid on the front panel.
"Unknown Artifact"
Radioactive. Almost nothing is known about the artifact's properties.
"Sample No. 768"
An organic artifact. Composed of deformed animal tissues. When exposed to chemical contamination zones, it "extracts" chemicals from the air, converting them into a thick viscous mass.
"Sample No. 815"
It has been established that radiation pulses from the thickened parts of this sample cause blood in open wounds to clot very quickly.
"Sample No. 862"
An organic artifact with unique properties. Somehow increases overall healing speed after injuries of any nature, without accelerating toxin buildup.
"Sample No. 874"
An artifact of electrostatic nature. Capable of sharply increasing the muscle tone of the wearer.
"Sample No. 895"
Generates a sphere of directed gravitational field around itself. Very effectively reduces the weight of the wearerÆs backpack contents.
"Sample No. 934"
Organic artifact. Generates an invisible sphere around itself that boosts the wearerÆs immunity.
"PDA with Assault Rifle Modification Data"

Contains detailed instructions for repair and upgrade of various mechanisms, such as the long-rod trigger system, roller-delayed blowback semi-free bolt system, and more. Data for weapons like AEK971, Famas F1, Colt M4A1.
"Flash Drive with Power Armor Modification Data"

This storage device contains information on installing additional modular systems that provide high levels of protection against radiation and psychic effects on the wearer, such as the army insulating armor "Gagarin" and the heavy stalker armor "Voskhod".
"Folder with Sniper Rifle Modification Data"
Contains detailed descriptions of improvements to muzzle brakes, barrels, and electronic stabilizers for sniper rifles: Barrett M82, CheyTac Intervention M200, and KSVK.

"Damn it! Beetle left no other clue but this piece of paper, so donÆt blame me. When you find it in the stashŚeat it right away... Just kidding. Now seriously. Archer fulfilled his part of the dealŚretrieved the cargo. Seems he did it cleanly. DidnÆt bring any tail, I hope. I checked the caseŚall as agreed. I left it at the VNZ KrugŚas always. You can take it, weÆll settle the rest tomorrow. Damn, whatÆs that ringing in my ears, now IÆll check... cont..."
"Note from Maypon"
"A very heavy case with unknown cargo. Seems itÆs not radioactive and doesnÆt cause headaches. Possibly some kind of device or part of equipment."
"MayponÆs Case"
"Folder with Blueprints"
This folder contains various information and blueprints needed to upgrade grenades.
"Special Tool Set"
Includes various tools necessary for assembling grenades into a cluster.
"PhantomÆs PDA"
PDA of the stalker nicknamed Phantom. All personal notes and information are under special protection.
"Cargo for Varg"
A fairly heavy crate. Contents unknown. Intended for the Duty saboteur Varg.
"Data Carrier for Laboratory X1"

Main laboratory, the most important facility of the "O-Consciousness" project. It was the largest scientific complex. Its task was to control the noosphere (the studied project) and modify it if possible. Control was to be carried out directly by a Scientific Staff member trained in Laboratory X6 (all who participated directly in the X1 and X2 experiments), in stasis. It consisted of 6 devices, prototypes located in Laboratory X16. They performed the main work, each ensuring sequential noosphere control during scientistsÆ consciousness implantation into the information shell. Laboratory X1 was located on the Chernobyl NPP territory. An underground complex near the unfinished 6th power unit was chosen. After construction was halted, underground rooms remained, which had to be completed but were relatively convenient for the laboratory."
"Data Carrier for Laboratory X2"

Also located on the NPP territory, directly connected with Laboratory X1, but situated under the 4th power unit. It housed scientific staff who conducted experiments to study and modify the noosphere. To cover the labs, an energy installation was created within the station to alter the consciousness of all under its control. This installation was soon called Monolith. It was reprogrammed many times to create a more advanced program. After the accident in Laboratory X1, attempts were made in X2 to fix the situation and take control, but unsuccessfully. Later, when armed forces appeared at Chernobyl NPP, measures were taken to alter consciousness of everyone in the area using the Monolith. This was the first human experiment, but surprisingly, not everyone came under controlŚthose who didnÆt died. When the experiment succeeded, the program worked, and people were made like guards for the complex, named after the Monolith device. Over time Monolith improved and reached full power, able to control Monolith forces."
"Data Carrier for Laboratory X6"

Laboratory complex X6 was the center for training scientific staff to manage the noosphere. It was a relatively small lab on the outskirts of the former city Limansk. Unlike other labs, it was not meant for long-term work, so after training, operations ceased and staff were transferred to X1. Its location required no choice: before the 1986 accident, a research station "Radiovolna" operated there, with an antenna nearby unrelated to the experiment. Necessary experimental equipment was also there, origins unclear to staff. The X6 lab program was closed in 2001.
"Data Carrier for Laboratory X7"

This scientific complex developed technical equipment for all labs. The chief designer was Professor S.I. Kaimanov. Thanks to his work, devices like the "Duga" radar and "Control" SVI were designed. These were intended for various labs and further experiments. The lab was in the buildings of the former "Jupiter" factory. The location was chosen because existing equipment and conditions suited work. The lab operated until the last 2006 accident at the X1 complex. Afterwards, no news about the X1 complex; personnel likely perished during the accident.
"Data Carrier for Laboratory X8"

The research and engineering complex X8 was one of the main labs of the "O-Consciousness" project. Many scientists worked here; it also trained people who would participate in X1 and X2 experiments. Trainees went to labs X6, X1, or X2 depending on direction. Engineers also trained here, later sent to other labs including X9. The complex was mostly a retraining center for specialists before their deployment. Other personnel worked on engineering development. The last engineer decision from X8 was to create from the "Duga" radar a device to reduce aggression near antennas. The engineering complex was led by senior researcher Professor N.A. Lebedev. The lab was closed in 2003 for unknown reasons; personnel were dispersed to various labs. Some went to X12, others (mostly engineers) to X9.
"Data Carrier for Laboratory X9"

A little-known complex staffed partly by scientists from secret defense research institutes rather than their original groups. Their goal was to create superweapons based on energy elements. Their only development was "Item No. 62", known as the "Gauss Gun". The project was stalled for a long time due to battery flaws. Though partly successful, it consumed too much power for battery charging. The project was closed in 2004; some personnel returned to defense institutes, others sent to other labs. Project leaders were sent to X1 with weapon samples for further development.
"Data Carrier for Laboratory X10"

Scientific complex initially represented by transmitter towers, but later transformed after changes in X8 and eventual conversion of underground storage into Laboratory X10. After reequipment, X10 became an aggression suppression station. The antennas were built on a small area of a former construction plant. The project was partially controlled by a secret defense research institute. Early experiments yielded disappointing results, the installation failed its objectives. After commission and observers left, the project continued with renewed efforts. Extensive work adjusted the antennas toward intended operation. In 2006, during a test, a second NPP accident caused the last tower antenna to tilt, resulting in equipment failure and the emergence of a psi-barrier intended to suppress aggression but instead amplified it, driving all under its influence insane. The first affected were all personnel working there. Over time, the installation was nicknamed "Brain Burner" for its zombifying effect within the complex.
"Data Carrier for Laboratory X12"

Essentially, this complex was not a real complex but a fa?ade to divert attention from commissions of other institutes. It served as a large archive for all documentation of the "O-Consciousness" project. Located on the former "Agroprom" research institute territory, staffed partly by personnel transferred from various labs. Its activity was limited to collecting documents and materials on conducted research and experiments. Fortunately, before the second NPP catastrophe, all primary documents were removed outside the Exclusion Zone; other materials were destroyed. After the accident, scientists did not return to X12, leaving the lab abandoned.
"Data Carrier for Laboratory X16"

This laboratory complex was one of the most important in the "O-Consciousness" project. Many staff worked here conducting experiments on the SVI "Control" installation. The project was complex: prototype development, building the lab and installation took much time and effort. The installation started working only in 1996. The main element caused overheating, requiring constant cooling supervision. Frequent malfunctions and technical design errors were observed, too late to fix. The installation consumed much energy, causing network overloads requiring constant attention. Staff complained of headaches attributed to the installation. During the NPP accident, a power surge caused irreversible damage in X16; everyone present lost their minds, zombified, or died.
"Data Carrier for Laboratory X18"

The last complex mentioned in the "O-Consciousness" project documents. Created when project leaders aimed to build the "Monolith". To maintain its state, living functional matter capable of various functions such as power support was needed. Since the installation was created using the noosphere, it required such elementsŚenergy clusters obtained and created by different means. This source was proven inexhaustible. Energy was transformed using special devices designed by Laboratory X7's Professor Kaimanov. Eventually, living organisms were used as the most reliable element, sent in special incubators to Lab X2 for integration into Monolith's control complex. After filling the cells and studying stability, the decision was made to halt further production. Equipment was conserved, staff transferred to Lab X7 until new energy cells would be needed.
"Directive on Objects One and Two of the S.T.A.L.K.E.R. Project"

Kharon. The experiment likely succeeded. Results should be expected soon. Per Krechet's directive: Object #1Śalso known as ShooterŚmust be delivered to the checkpoint at Cordon; Object #2Śknown as ShramŚmust be delivered to the checkpoint near Military, close to the Barrier. Use the death truck and two reliable mercenaries for delivery. After operation completion, all witnesses including executors must be eliminated. Nothing should stop us now...
"Thermal Anomaly Scanner"
Specialized anomaly activity scanner, model series 18.4798. Used by scientists to study processes occurring in thermal anomalies.
"Psi-Static Anomaly Scanner"
Specialized anomaly activity scanner, model series 16.4451. Used by scientists to study processes occurring in psi-static anomalies.
"Gravitational Symbiont Scanner"
Specialized anomaly activity scanner, model series 14.2321. Used by scientists to study processes occurring in gravitational anomalies.
"Chemical Anomaly Scanner"
Specialized anomaly activity scanner, model series 12.1150. Used by scientists to study processes occurring in chemical anomalies.
"Zubr's PDA"

"PDA of the stalker-hunter named Zubr. Last entry: 'Observing those creatures southwest of Sosnodub. About ten individuals behaving aggressively but not attacking each other. Strange. Their behavior resembles Chernobyl dogs more than ordinary blind ones. And their looks Ś like Cerberus but with one head. Need to get closer, maybe I can quietly take one down...'"
"Professor Kalancha's PDA"

"Last entry: 'Could we really have made a miscalculation? If so, we're all doomed here. Neither Shram nor anyone else will help now. We should have tried to eliminate Strelok earlier. But now it's pointless... Monolith followers are everywhere... They killed Gandalf, that's it... this is the end...'"
"Professor Lebedev's PDA"

"Last entry: 'Shram got to Strelok too late. I doubt it will change anything now. Our forces are running out. This decision was suicide, even if we had ten times more fighters... ItÆs already too late... Shram broke through Strelok's psi-shield... but what is this... an emission... no... what... aa... '"
"Directive on Objects Three and Four of the S.T.A.L.K.E.R. Project"

"Guron. The experiment likely succeeded. Results should be expected soon. Per Krechet's directive: Objects #3 and #4, also known as Lebedev and Kalancha, must be transported to the Tesla facility for further processing. All traces of their presence in the lab must be destroyed. Personal belongings of the objects should be delivered to the surface of Pripyat. Create a stash near the river port close to your base. They may still be useful..."
"Schematic of the Reduced Gauss Coil"
"A set of blueprints and schematics for the main component of an experimental pistol ¢ the Gauss coil. Without it, creating the necessary parts for the Gauss pistol is unlikely."
"Item #58"
"Non-working prototype of a Gauss pistol."
"Box of Transistors"
"Ordinary electronic components Ś field-effect transistors. Required to build the Gauss coil."
"Gauss Coil"

"A reduced version of the Gauss coil used in the weapon of the same name. Necessary for generating the electromagnetic field of the accelerator Ś the core component of both the Gauss rifle and Gauss pistol."
"Package with Cargo"
"A small compact polyethylene package containing white powder. Could be heroin or something else."
"Case with Cargo"
"By weight, it seems to be a sizable batch of narcotics. Worth at least half a million."
"Flash Drive with Files"
"Several text files containing names, phone numbers, and addresses. Looks like a database of couriers and middlemen in a criminal network. Not much use to me Ś IÆm not in narcotics control."
"Case with Nanokevlar"
"Sturdy, convenient, fireproof case containing cargo belonging to scientists Ś a unique nanokevlar."
"Schematic of Servomotors and Hydraulic Boosters"
"Highly valuable information necessary for technicians working with both hydraulic boosters and servomotors."
"Flash Drive with Data"
"Professor Mironov's flash drive containing copies of files on the creation of semi-invisible items."
"Case with Documents"
"Professor Mironov's case containing documents and research results in the field of partial mimicry."
"Kanevsky's PDA"
"Dolg operative Kanevsky's PDA. All information is locked."
"Folder with Kanevsky's Notes"

"Space on Yanov is running out. 'Freedom' is too firmly entrenched here, and we have too few men to try to push them out. Voronin refuses to send reinforcements Ś they have something similar going on, mutants pouring from the Dark Valley by the masses, plus mercenaries trying to hold the bar. It's bad. Varg said we need to look for new zones of influence. ThereÆs no better place than Zaton. Scouts report that all possible spots for a stronghold are taken by loners. They wonÆt leave peacefully, and you canÆt drive them out by force Ś donÆt need loners as enemies too. ThereÆs an abandoned ship on Zaton where stalkers set up a base. A local trader called Sych runs things there, bartenderÆs nickname is 'Beard'. We tried to negotiate. Promised them everything Ś money, artifacts, supplies from Dolg warehouses Ś but no deal. TheyÆre experienced bastards, not to be fooled. They decided theyÆre better off on their own. We gathered a council and decided: hire bandits to handle the loners Ś theyÆve lost all fear. In 2-3 days they should attack Skadovsk and clear out all the stalker scum. Then, a week or two later, when loners cry out, Sultan will crush them hard. WeÆll have the 'liberation' march, wipe out the bandits, and Dolg will have a base in Skadovsk."
"Folder with Kanevsky's Documents"

"Started negotiations with bandits. They agreed, glad to finally get revenge. At first, they didnÆt believe us Ś suspicious after all the shooting Ś but then fully supported us and even brought gear on a silver platter. We convinced them the Zone is changing, plans change, former enemies become best friends. There are two of us from the Zaton HQ Ś me and Morgan. Morgan manages our warehouses and arranges supply of gear to bandits. The deal was supposed to happen already, they met at the Forestry. IÆm negotiating with SultanÆs ambassador. Sultan himself is a bastard, afraid to show up. IÆve held three meetings with his rep, Kaban. What a jerk. We scheduled a fourth meeting. Yesterday we waited and saw not Kaban, but another bandit Ś called himself Avenger Ś say that Kaban was killed by loners and now heÆs chief negotiator for the bandits. Also said Morgan was shot dead by stalkers at the Forestry during the deal. Those bastards found out somehow! Still, the deal was made, and tomorrow AvengerÆs rep will bring a suitcase of money to pay for gear. He canÆt come himself, suspects free stalkers are onto them. WeÆll wait."
"Ammo Crate"
"A very heavy crate packed to the brim with cartridges."
"Kanevsky's PDA"
"PDA belonging to Dolg operative Kanevsky. Data is password protected and encrypted."
"Teuton's PDA"

"Last message: 'SerbinÆs group died, but according to new data X8 is declassified. XavierÆs group was supposed to back them up, but IÆm not sure they managed what Serbin couldnÆt. Go there and extract everything from the lab that can point to the "Source" project.'"
"Xavier's PDA"
"Last message: 'Fagot, mission complete. We have the data on the "Source" project. Set up a meeting with Richards. Preferably somewhere remote to avoid prying eyes.'"
"Captain Richards' PDA"

"Last message: 'The mercenary contacted us. Xavier will wait near the plant, not far from Yanov station. I hope the meeting wonÆt take long. If something goes wrong, my teams will kill Xavier and retrieve the documents. After that, our mission will be complete.'"
"Case with Documentation"
"Case containing information about the 'Source' project."
"Mirovoy's PDA"

"Last entry: We identified what the sinners use. Some new drug. I donÆt know what it does to the psyche. The sinners were unloading large quantities near the container warehouses. Their leader who brought the drug was called Demonologist. He mentioned to another man named Osiris that the next batch would arrive in a few days. Damn, are those Controllers? Looks like psionics... damn, weÆre out, they spotted us..."
"Demonologist's PDA"
"Last entry: Osiris, we need to retreat. Lead most of the people to Jupiter, I will join you soon. This is just the beginning. The Inspirer is already here. The Masters know what to do..."
"Osiris' Documents"

"Entry: It looks like we were simply used. We mean nothing to the Masters. Anubis is their watchdog, ready to go all the way. What he wants is unclear. And it no longer matters. Greh has fallen, but they will pay for it. And Anubis first of all..."
